records.ety: [F. bergamote, fr. It. bergamotta; prob. a corruption of Turk. beg armūdi a lord's pear.]

defs: A tree of the Orange family (Citrus bergamia), having a roundish or pear-shaped fruit, from the rind of which an essential oil of delicious odor is extracted, much prized as a perfume. Also, the fruit.
A variety of mint (Mentha aquatica, var. glabrata).
The essence or perfume made from the fruit.
A variety of snuff perfumed with bergamot.
quotes: The better hand . . . gives the nose its bergamot.

defs: A coarse tapestry, manufactured from flock of cotton or hemp, mixed with ox's or goat's hair; -- said to have been invented at Bergamo, Italy. Encyc. Brit.
notes: Oil of bergamot is used as a flavoring agent in the popular "Earl Gray's tea".
